dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All six line-item requirements are satisfied: Noto_Sans_SC imported and configured correctly with subsets:['latin'], notoSansSC.variable applied to <html>, mandarinWord changed from 24px to 36px with --font-noto-sans-sc, pinyin and English labels untouched, CSS variable available at render time via Next.js font optimization, TypeScript compiles clean. mascot.tsx created matching spec exactly.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: N/A for this task — no async operations, API calls, or data fetching were introduced. Existing error handling (image onError fallback in VocabularyCard) is untouched. -1 is a placeholder for the inherent limitation that font-load failures (e.g. offline) fall back to system sans-serif via display:swap, which is correct behaviour and not a bug.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10:
Font renders at the correct size with appropriate leading-tight class added (bonus improvement preventing runaway line-height at 36px). FOUT is prevented by Next.js injecting the CSS variable server-side. Card stays within max-w-sm on 375px viewport — CJK characters wrap naturally in a text-center flex column. -1: no overflow guard (e.g. overflow-wrap:break-word or break-all) on the Mandarin

for hypothetical very long vocabulary entries, but for realistic 1-4 character vocabulary words this is a non-issue.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Minimal diff, consistent with existing inline-style pattern for fontFamily already used throughout VocabularyCard.tsx. All three files are well under size limits (layout.tsx: 49 lines, VocabularyCard.tsx: 58 lines, mascot.tsx: 14 lines). The leading-tight addition is a sensible bonus. -1: the mascot.tsx fontSize prop passes a raw number to style.fontSize which React renders as px — this is correct React behaviour but slightly implicit; a comment or string type would be marginally clearer. Not a defect.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Existing 7 VocabularyCard tests all pass (they assert text content only, not CSS properties, so font/size changes don't affect them). No new tests added for mascot.tsx — acceptable for a trivial single-element emoji component, but a basic render smoke test would be best practice. -2 for that gap.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or credentials. No user input paths introduced. No LLM prompt changes. No auth-sensitive routes touched. Full marks.

summary: Clean, minimal implementation that satisfies every line-item requirement. Noto_Sans_SC is correctly imported, configured, and wired into the CSS variable chain in layout.tsx. The mandarinWord element in VocabularyCard.tsx is updated to 36px with --font-noto-sans-sc, while pinyin (16px / Nunito) and English word (36px / Fredoka) remain untouched. The leading-tight class is a sensible bonus that prevents line-height blowout. mascot.tsx matches the task spec exactly at 14 lines. No P1 issues found. The only minor gap is the absence of a smoke test for the new mascot.tsx file, which is P2 at most given the component's trivial nature. Verdict: pass.
